what: `zap-baseline.yml` is a GitHub Actions CI workflow (component-inventory file path `.github/workflows/zap-baseline.yml`, Phase 2) that runs an OWASP ZAP **baseline passive** DAST scan against the application on every pull request. It is configured to invoke the pinned action `zaproxy/action-baseline@v0.12.0` with the input `fail_action: true`, so the workflow (and therefore the PR) fails if the baseline scan surfaces findings. Its status is registered under the CI check name `zap-baseline`, which is mandated to be a REQUIRED status check at merge. Scope is explicitly limited to the baseline passive scan; active/full ZAP scans are OUT OF SCOPE for v1. It is the single concrete artifact for Req…

open_gaps.gap: Baseline-block collision: Requirement 31 (DAST) is tagged to baseline block B15, but B15 is defined as 'Durable storage' (mapped to Requirement 16 / REQ-STORE-001..003). A DAST security scan is mis-filed under the storage block in BOTH the traceability matrix (line 418) and the requirement header (line 487).
open_gaps.fix: Reassign Requirement 31 / REQ-SEC-008 to a security baseline block (e.g. the SAST/secrets/SLSA security family near B16/B17 used by the kill-switch, or a dedicated security block). Update both requirements.md:418 (matrix) and requirements.md:487 (header) to the corrected block, and verify the corrected block is defined in the baseline-block list (requirements.md:397).

open_gaps.gap: Missing wiring to the merge gate: REQ-SEC-008.2 mandates `zap-baseline` be a REQUIRED status check, but it is absent from the canonical required-status-checks enumeration in docs/github-ruleset.md (listed at tasks.md:270, which names only formal-verification, coverage-gate, secrets-scan, audit-chain-verify + human reviewer). Sibling required checks each have a task-40.x subtask that creates the workflow AND adds it to the ruleset (40.2 secrets-scan, 40.3 audit-chain-verify); zap-baseline has no such subtask — task 56 says 'register' with no registration mechanism. deepeval-gate (REQ-EVAL-001) has the identical omission.
open_gaps.fix: Add `zap-baseline` (and `deepeval-gate`) to the required-status-checks list at tasks.md:270, and add a task-40.x subtask (e.g. 40.4) that creates `.github/workflows/zap-baseline.yml` and registers `zap-baseline` in the GitHub repository ruleset + docs/github-ruleset.md, mirroring task 40.3 for audit-chain-verify.

open_gaps.gap: No `on:` trigger specified: REQ-SEC-008.1 says 'on every PR' in prose, but no canonical file defines an `on: pull_request` block, branch filter, or event scope. The literal trigger appears nowhere (grep-confirmed).
open_gaps.fix: Specify the workflow trigger in the design row or task 56, e.g. `on: pull_request` (optionally with target-branch filter for the protected branch), matching how secrets-scan/property-tests workflows describe their PR triggers (tasks.md:707).

open_gaps.gap: Scan target undefined: nothing specifies WHAT ZAP scans — no target URL, no ephemeral app stand-up, no docker-compose/localhost target, no auth context. The rationale even concedes 'active scan added post-v1 when an authenticated target exists' (requirements.md:490), implying even the baseline target is unsettled. A DAST scan with no defined target cannot run.
open_gaps.fix: Define the v1 baseline scan target in design.md (e.g. spin up the app in CI against a known localhost URL, or scan a deployed preview/ephemeral environment) and document how the action's target input is populated. Without this, task 56 is not implementable as written.

open_gaps.gap: Design thinness: this component exists ONLY as (a) a single 1-line component-inventory row (design.md:150), (b) the 3-clause EARS block (requirements.md:486-490), and (c) one tasks.md task (976) with no subtasks. design.md provides NO detail on trigger, target, report/SARIF handling, artifact retention, failure semantics, or ruleset wiring. Critically, the design.md 'New / changed components' Phase-2 reconciliation list (design.md:1179-1185) — which DOES enumerate the other Phase-2 P3 additions (state_integrity, audit_log/audit_verify, perf_a11y_verifier, REASONING span, research fact-check) — OMITS zap-baseline entirely, as well as deepeval_gate, flagd, and omission_guard. The component is …
open_gaps.fix: Add a design.md subsection (under 'New / changed components' or a CI-workflows section) describing zap-baseline's trigger, scan target, fail_action semantics, report handling, and ruleset registration, parallel to the treatment given to audit_log/audit_verify (design.md:1168-1181). At minimum, add zap-baseline (and deepeval_gate, flagd, omission_guard) to the design.md:1179 'New / changed components' list so the Phase-2 inventory is complete.

open_gaps.gap: 'DAST' acronym used but never defined in the Glossary (requirements.md:15-48), unlike the related security terms Semgrep_CodeQL and SLSA which ARE defined. Used in the Requirement 31 title and criterion 31.2.
open_gaps.fix: Add a Glossary entry: 'DAST: Dynamic Application Security Testing — black-box scanning of a running application; here the OWASP ZAP baseline passive scan (REQ-SEC-008).' at requirements.md:48.

open_gaps.gap: No formal-verification coverage and no statement that this is intentional. REQ-SEC-008 has no Property and no Z3 CHECK (grep-confirmed in formal_verification_merged.py). While consistent with peer CI gates, the spec never declares REQ-SEC-008 as a [DET]-only obligation outside the Z3 harness scope, leaving ambiguity about whether the absence is intentional or an oversight.
open_gaps.fix: Annotate Requirement 31 as a [DET] CI-gate obligation explicitly outside the formal-verification harness scope (as is implicitly true for secrets-scan, coverage-gate, deepeval-gate), so the absence of a CHECK is documented intent rather than a gap. Add a one-line note at requirements.md:486.

open_gaps.gap: The physical artifact does not exist: `.github/workflows/zap-baseline.yml` is not present in the repo (find confirmed no such file; .github/workflows/ directory yields nothing for zap), and task 56 is an unchecked `[ ]` item. This is expected for a spec-stage component, but combined with the missing ruleset registration and undefined target, the REQUIRED-check claim (31.2) is currently unbacked by any buildable mechanism.
open_gaps.fix: Track as not-yet-built; ensure the build task (56) and a ruleset-registration subtask are both completed before REQ-SEC-008.2 can be claimed satisfied. Cross-link task 56 to the docs/github-ruleset.md update.

critic_findings.detail: The draft behavior 'Participate in merge-gate security set' asserts zap-baseline 'acts as the DAST member of the CI / Merge-Gate security-check set alongside OPA/Conftest, Semgrep+CodeQL (SAST), secrets-scan, and SLSA provenance' and cites design.md:50. Independently verified: the design.md CI subgraph (design.md:50-55) lists ONLY four nodes — OPA, SAST (Semgrep+CodeQL), SLSA, GH_RULESET. Neither zap-baseline NOR secrets-scan appears in that diagram, and grep finds no prose anywhere naming a 'security-check set' or 'DAST member.' The cited line does not support a membership/co-participation claim; zap-baseline is not wired into any depicted gate set. The behavior overstates the spec's actual…

Cross-phase required-check timing contradiction the draft does not flag. Task 56 (zap-baseline) is a Phase 2 component (design.md:150; dependency-graph wave id 47, tasks.md:931). But ALL required-status-check CI wiring is done under task 40 'Integrate ... as required CI check (Phase 4 hardening)' (tasks.md:689), whose subtasks 40.1/40.2/40.3 create-and-register the formal-verification/secrets-scan/audit-chain-verify checks and are listed together in the dependency graph at tasks.md:875. Because zap-baseline has no 40.x subtask, its REQUIRED-check registration (REQ-SEC-008.2) is not scheduled in ANY phase — the Phase-2 build task cannot itself register a Phase-4 ruleset gate. The draft's open…

deepeval-gate (REQ-EVAL-001, task 55) is also absent from the canonical required-status-checks enumeration at tasks.md:270 and also lacks a task-40.x create-and-register subtask — identical to the zap-baseline omission. The draft mentions deepeval-gate's parallel omission only in passing inside one open_gap's suggested_fix and never verifies it as a standalone confirmed defect. Independently confirmed: tasks.md:270 names only formal-verification, coverage-gate, secrets-scan, audit-chain-verify + human reviewer; tasks.md:484 mandates 'deepeval-gate' as a REQUIRED check at merge but tasks.md:975 (task 55) gives it no 40.x wiring. This corroborates the draft's missing-wiring finding for zap-bas…

Property-count inconsistency adjacent to the draft's 'no Property covers this component' claim. The draft's properties_and_checks section asserts the canonical set is 'Properties 1-30 (design.md:1034-1124).' But tasks.md:732 states 'The 29 correctness properties in Phase 4 ... Properties 1-24 from the original phases; Properties 25-29 authored under tasks 48-52' (i.e. 29, ending at Property 29), while requirements.md:514, the omission_guard rows (design.md:148, design.md:218), and the Property block header (design.md:1066 onward through Property 30) treat it as 30 properties including Property 30 (omission-declaration). The draft never flags that the property-count is itself contradictory be…

The draft's traceability/header B15 gap is correct but understates the verification value of the FIX target. The draft suggests reassigning REQ-SEC-008 to 'a security baseline block (e.g. ... near B16/B17 used by the kill-switch).' Independently verified: B16 is 'Supply-chain & security gates' mapped to Requirement 17 / REQ-SEC-001..005 (requirements.md:398), which is the precise correct home for a DAST security scan, whereas B17 is 'Human-in-the-loop approval' (requirements.md:399) and is NOT a security-scan block — the kill-switch (Req 32) is dual-tagged B16/B17 only because it spans gates+approval. The draft's 'B16/B17' phrasing risks mis-tagging REQ-SEC-008 to B17; the correct single tar…

ears_needing_work.how: The REQ-SEC-008 PRD mapping is correct, but the baseline-block assignment B15 is wrong: B15 is defined as 'Durable storage' (requirements.md:397, mapped to Requirement 16 / REQ-STORE-001..003). A DAST security scan does not belong to the durable-storage block — this is a mis-tagged citation / incons…
